talk_title: Keynotes 5 — David Hsu, Stefanie Tellex, Noémie Jaquier, Paolo Robuffo Giordano
speaker: David Hsu; Stefanie Tellex; Noémie Jaquier; Paolo Robuffo Giordano
tc_relevance_reason: The TC-bullseye keynote block of the conference. Hsu on planning under uncertainty (DESPOT, POMDP planning); Jaquier on geometry-aware learning for control; Robuffo Giordano on multi-robot control and teleoperation. Three out of four speakers are direct TC scope at senior level. Tellex on language-grounded planning fills the fourth slot. If you attend one keynote block all week, this is it.

speaker: Roland Siegwart
tc_relevance_reason: Siegwart is one of the senior figures in autonomous mobile robotics and field deployment — co-author of the standard mobile robotics textbook, longtime leader at ETH ASL. Plenary topic is broad-vision but the perspective on where mobile robotics is going from someone who has shaped the field for decades is worth the slot.

talk_title: Keynotes 6 — Julia A. Adams, Marcia O'Malley, Tetsunari Inamura, Berk Calli
speaker: Julia A. Adams; Marcia O'Malley; Tetsunari Inamura; Berk Calli
speaker_affiliation: Oregon State; Rice; Tokyo Metropolitan; WPI
tc_relevance_reason: HRI / haptics / simulation / grasping lineup. TC-adjacent — Adams on multi-agent HRI and Calli on grasping have the closest TC ties but the block leans more applied/HRI than core planning-and-control. Worth a look if these subfields touch your work.
